text.title: Go error handling: wrapping, linting, and agent rules
text.summary: Wrapping discipline, linting enforcement (errcheck, errorlint, wrapcheck), and AI-agent-specific gotchas for Go error handling.

rule.statement: Wrap at every layer boundary using fmt.Errorf("<layer> <op>: %w", err). The prefix identifies the caller in the error chain for SRE debugging.
rule.rationale: An unwrapped error loses its call-site context. A chain of "user lookup: order fetch: db scan: ..." tells on-call exactly which layer failed without opening the profiler.

rule.statement: Never call Wrap(err, msg) on a known domain error (validation, not-found). Use typed constructors (NewNotFound, AsValidation) so the HTTP status is preserved. Wrap is for unexpected/internal errors only.
rule.rationale: Wrap always returns HTTPStatus 500. Applying it to a 404 downgrades the status, breaking client error handling.

rule.statement: Never embed secrets, SQL queries, or PII in AppError.Message. These bubble to clients via middleware. Use apperror.Internal(err) to swap the public message and keep the chain for server-side logging.
rule.rationale: Error messages that reach clients are part of the API contract and may appear in browser network tabs or mobile app crash logs.

rule.statement: Enable errcheck, errorlint, wrapcheck, and nilerr in golangci-lint. These make un-typed errors, unwrapped cross-package errors, and if err != nil { return nil } mistakes fail CI.
rule.rationale: Manual code review misses missing wrappings; lint enforcement catches them at compile time, before the error reaches production.

list.item: wrapcheck — requires cross-package errors to be wrapped
nilerr — catches if err != nil { return nil } mistakes
staticcheck SA4006 / SA1019 — misc error misuse

antipattern.description: Returning raw err.Error() to clients: c.JSON(400, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
antipattern.reason: Leaks database DSNs, query text, internal hostnames. Always intercept in middleware and return a safe AppError.Message.

antipattern.description: Conflating errors.Is (sentinel match) and errors.As (type assertion).
antipattern.reason: errors.Is checks identity/equality down the chain; errors.As checks type. Using Is on a struct pointer that was reconstructed always returns false.

antipattern.description: Using Wrap() as a generic wrapper on every error including user errors.
antipattern.reason: Wrap always produces 500. User-facing errors (400, 404, 409) must use typed constructors that carry the correct status.
